response.justification: The role has a hard minimum of 8 years of C++ experience, which is the single largest disqualifying factor: the resume contains only a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments writing C/C++ components and a bachelor thesis reference, with C++ absent entirely from the listed skills. Beyond that critical gap, the candidate has no demonstrated cloud platform leadership, no formal technical lead role, and infrastructure exposure is limited to a microservices stint at Sociomantic and some Docker/CI work at Altom — far short of the 5-year large-scale infrastructure requirement. The candidate's actual strengths (TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, TDD advocacy, full-stack breadth) are simply not what this role demands.
strengths: Strong TDD and software craftsmanship culture with consistent application across multiple roles
Demonstrated experience building high-throughput distributed microservices (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic)
Broad polyglot background and history of owning ambiguous projects end-to-end (GISCollective, open-source tooling)
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI + thesis) versus the 8-year hard minimum — the most critical requirement is essentially unmet
No formal technical lead, engineering manager, or cloud architect role on record; leadership appears informal and team-level at best
Cloud architecture depth is shallow: AWS CDK usage at Tencent is the only cloud reference; no evidence of designing or owning a large-scale compute or infrastructure platform
